reviews.review_text:
Library of ruina has become one of my favorite games so far, both story and gameplay-wise.
I initially found the deck-building part of the game a bit tedious, but it really grew on me over the time! you get a lot of freedom when it comes to passives and combat pages, especially as you progress through the game.
I also loved how gimmicky boss battles are. Each abnormality fight is basically just a big angry puzzle that's trying to kill you.

The art and soundtrack are both pretty good. Even if Mili gets the spotlight, the game does have quite a few bangers besides them I believe! (Kether and Netzach floor theme.. oughh <3)
I would strongly suggest to play Lobotomy Corporation or at least watch a video on it first to fully enjoy the story and the characters.
Overall, Library of Ruina is an amazing deck-building game about change and what it means to be human. I'd definitly recommend if you are willing to dedicate some time to learn the gameplay! :3c
i usually write funny reviews but i cant, that white ♥♥♥♥♥ boy is beating my ass and i cant finish the game

WTF is this game? - Gameplay's a bit akward at first.. but the depth of the story and strategy is insane.

Wild concepts. Heart rending insight.
If you don't mind staring into the mouth of madness...
Worth playing.
If you have patience or have seen the good stuff already, you'll see that this game is absolute cinema. Genuine peak of fiction.

Slay the spire but you play as the spire. 

Highly addictive, both in story and in gameplay.

I stopped larping and finally beat this game although suffered doing so, was worth it in the end but at what cost. 

Netzach best character.
[spoiler] Foreword ending SUCKS [/spoiler]

This game took me a while to finish after Lob Corp, but what a ride it was. One of the most in depth strategic phenomenal JRPGs I've ever played, and also one of the best deck builders I've ever seen. A story that had me in tears, music that I listen to on repeat, and a game that STRESSED ME OUT. Game hard, game good, game fun. Cannot recommend it enough to anyone who wants a strategic game that actually challenges them, Roland deserved his Hamham Pangpang godamnit.

10/10 game. amazing music, fun and challenging gameplay and the absolute Cinema of a story. a game i consider one of the greats of all time. if you have interest in this game i highly suggest you play it blind to enjoy the story

A game that expresses a beautiful story with a spiking difficulty in gameplay every chapter. Mods have helped make the game more tolerable however and it really lays out the setting of the city. A good setter before going into Limbus Company or a way of learning more about the City if you've already played Limbus Company to its entirety.
even tho the game difficulty is vertical i still would recommed because of the baddies

if you like Limbus Company, you'll sort off won't like Ruina at the beginning, but once you get the hang of how stuff works, it's a very road-bumpy experience that will require to READ, AHHHH!!!!!
Overall is a game. Not for everyone (me included, I'm not a fan of deckbuilders.) but it's indeed a game set in the Project Moon universe. 6/10
This game is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ peak, but the difficulty is vertical.
This game is perfect in almost all aspects, seriously, if you ever doubted paying for this, pay it and play. 10/10 game
difficult  but very fun!!! got it after playing limbus and I want to do things to pierre

Amazing game, I love the combat, the characters, the story, the music, the art style and aesthetic. It's perfect and you can play it whether you've played the game that came before it, Lobotomy Corporation, or not. I don't know what to say about it other than play the game for yourself if you haven't already.

definitely PM's best work to date. the gameplay is engaging and allows for player strategy in ways the other games don't, along the way providing the best stories in the city, phillip, yan, xiao, and the library itself. 100% a must play for anyone a fan of deck builders or just interested after playing limbus company
Good story challenging when it needs to be great important memorable parts that immerse yourself into the world and lore. My only gripe is lack of being able to use rolands stuff in the finale
My favourite part of game was part when malktuh was moaning to ronald after defeating scorched child
Peak ost, peak characters, peak world, peak gameplay(blunt 12-20), peak silence, peak CEO, PEAK GAME

the worst feature about this game is the fact that it punishes you for exiting a level like tell me why i have to restart the game every single time i want to restart a reception? anyways amazing game worth the 30 bucks
Incredible story, Project Moon is incredible and everyone working there has done an absolutely fantastic job

I think this game is amazing, the characters all have charm to them, and the story is compelling. Some of the fights, particularly at the end of the game feel a bit like a slog, but overall I love this game, the combat and unique ways you can go about solving a problem just makes this game an easy 10/10 for me.

I'm a huge fan of turn based combat and strategy, and it's story and visuals are equally as beautiful. (I'm also incredibly attached to Project Moon as a whole)

I also enjoy how the battle system isn't reused in their other game, Limbus Company, which was admittedly my introduction, as this is a deck builder!

Overall, 10/10, I love PM, long live Kim

Despite not having any prior knowledge about project moon universe I was still able to completely understand the story(which I really enjoyed), THE MUSIC IN THIS GAME IS THE BEST GLORY TO MILI, and the gameplay, well... it's fun and deep but unless you are some deck building genius you will either spend a LOT of time on testing various decks to find actually good ones or (what I started doing at the end of SOTC) just look up deck guides, because the game is actually pretty easy if you use the META decks. Overall a great game especially for its price. GLORY TO KIM JI HOON
literally my favourite game of all time, i LOVE the art so much, the gameplay is done so, so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ well and the story. DONT GET ME STARTED ON THE STORY. ITS SO PEAKKK

Very good game, love the writing, but I'll have to come back and update my thoughts when I hit the "vertical difficulty cliff" I keep hearing about.

Update: Found the cliff. Oof. I hope you either like getting your ass kicked until you get gud, or are willing to fight bullsh*t with cheese. (Like stacking 25 bleed on a boss, and having them choke to death on their own blood because they trigger the bleed stack every red dice on their single-turn 8-different-attacks uber-death-combo.)
just finished the first floor realization and had to immediately pause the game and come do this.

One of my favorite games of all time. I'll start off with all the pro's/things I like then move on to the stuff I think could be down better. The story is expertly written. The arcs of the main 2 (Roland and Angela) are truly great, with both of them being some of my favorite characters of all time now. The side characters are also superb, but have less time put into them and as such aren't the main focus of the game. Even the enemies oftentimes have interesting stories that you may have even followed through half the game. Alongside that, the dialogue is excellent and all the conversations are fun to follow. The music is incredible, as expected of Mili and Project Moon really. Just listen to the intro on steam, most of the games music is very similar to that. The art is great and fits the universe well, though i wish there was more of the full splash art. The gameplay is probably my least favorite aspect, but by no means is it bad. It is still my favorite deck builder and I'm not even really a fan of deck builders. The build variety is so vast and all the enemies you fight all feel so different that no fights ever really feel the same (outside of the first few hours anyway). The issues with this game come from the difficulty and the pacing towards the end in my opinion. I don't think the game is unmanageably hard, but it is probably one of the hardest games I've ever played. The knowledge and patience required for some of the fights makes the game really exhausting, on top of the fact that the late game fights and realizations can last upwards of an hour sometimes. This can make the game very tiring to play when you're in a hour long fight and you die right at the end because you read a passive incorrectly. This kind of leads into the second point where the ending of the game is too long in my opinion. After the message where the game tells you there is no going back, I played around another 10 hours beating all the bosses (which number 12 not counting phase transitions). All the bosses are very fun and make you run different builds to beat them, it just feels like the pacing of the story slows down so much when I desperately want to see what happens next. Maybe I was just expecting it to be faster though because I knew some of what was going to happen, Idk. Still an incredible game though, solid 9.5/10 for me, probably would be higher if I was more of a deck builder fan.

If you're a PM larper, I promise you playing the actual game is still so worth it. You only really have the basic plot points of the story if you've only watched videos on the story. The conversations Roland has with all the librarians are so full of personality, and basically every bit of dialogue between 2+ characters actually sound like they're people talking to each other instead of voice actors talking in a booth into a microphone.

As for the combat, if you have any actual interest in PM games you know Ruina doesn't ♥♥♥♥ around, especially when getting to Urban Plague, especially the abnormalities. Play blind as much as you can, but don't let the difficult fights sour your experience by butting your head against it for days. It's alright to look at a guide if you are genuinely stuck at any point.

After 60 hours I have finally finished LoR, and, no survivors, everyone at Project moon needs to go to the P.E.C. for some of the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ in this game. Jokes aside they absolutely cooked with this game, the art and lore continues to be absolutely incredible. The gameplay manages to be a dice oriented combat system that still feels deeply strategic and complex without being overwhelming. The characters are super interesting and there's so many twists and turns I did not expect. I have a confession however, and please do forgive me, I beat this game modded...
I downloaded the no grind mod because I didn't want to grind for books. 

Phenomenal game with some BS difficulty and way too many instakills during some particularly egregious fights.

The perfect example of simple mechanics blending together to form a complex system that while being intricate, is still easily understandable. The early game does a wonderful job at teaching the basics of gameplay, slowly introducing mechanics that can build off each other, all well paced to allow the player to learn and get better. By the late stages of the game, they no longer hold back their punches and expects the player to be fully familiar with the gameplay loop. By far one of the best turn-based games out there, and definitely the toughest but most rewarding game I've played. Perfect for any strategy lover out for a tough challenge.

Reviewing the story would be an injustice to the absolute cinema that is this game. I will say nothing more than "Roland my goat."
This game is if you made a really amazing game with cool characters then attached ♥♥♥♥ and ball torture to the end game fights
Wow, this game excels in every single aspect from gameplay to art to story, Would recommend that everyonn try this game despite its harsh difficulty spikes.

I have only played untill love town (or the first love town as idk if there are more) and i have been having alot of fun!! This is my first deck builder game, where i have mostly played sandbox (minecraft, vintage story) and mmo eploration games (sky cotl) before this. I went in nearly fully blind with only having watched one project moon related video (watched shenpais Library of ruina video, it got me to want to play the game) and i think its balanced in how hard it is. Sure theres a dificulty spike but it happens when i started to get a bit bored of how could do everything on one try. So honestly its well placed. At least from what i can tell. ^-^

Sure most of the levels around love town level i have had to do multible attempts at (where most of the other levels incuding the red ones on the spefic library levels i have managed to first try like 65% ish of them), but when i lose i tend learn from the losses. After that i tend to win after 2 to 5 ish trys. After all what doesnt perma kill you only makes you stronger!!! Those losses acually just makes it even more satisfying when i win!! Like yay i defeated the hard to kill guy!!! Time to take their cards and become stronger!!!! : DDDDDD

Honestly just read, keep track of numbers, weaknesses and combinations of effect cards and things will go well!! ^-^
Wrote a thesis paper about the cynicism of the city and rejecting its cruelty by fighting against it. Thank you Project Moon for making me pass this class.

Library of Ruina is a game with too many flaws, but it has a special charm that i couldn't ignore

I've played Lobotomy Corporation, then came to play this, played for 10ish hours, and then dropped it
Played Limbus Company afterwards, loved the game, and came back to Library of Ruina

It was an awesome experience, like, absurdly incredible
The game's worldbuilding, overall PM worldbuilding is among the best that i've encountered in all of fiction in my opinion, the combat and the gameplay are both strong factors of the game, the deck building may seem like a chore sometimes, but you have a lot of options to counter that, even using an deck guide through each battle so you don't have much of a headache. The characters in this game are absolutely well done and incredible, game's story overall is amazing, and some moments make it feel worth in the end

Game's plot twists are pretty cool and the overall design of them is one of the big points of this game, songs are incredible, going from usual osts to actual songs made by Mili

Lots of the battles in this game are repetitive, boring, and take too much time, but other ones are absolutely incredible duels and show that this game can be incredible anytime

It has a lot of flaws anyways, some abnormality fights have unique and fun gimmicks, while others are simply boring and frustrating, a lot of receptions have too many enemies and makes you spend a lot of time for something that's not worth in the end, i think the relationship between some librarians and some guests could have been better utilized
After about 50 hours into the game, you start to get tired of too many fights and too many enemies, and it only gets harder and the fights only get longer
Game is a lot inconsistent overall, some parts are awesome, while for some time ur stuck with boring, then one incredible thing comes, then goes back to boring again, it's pretty random in that sense

Overall, i don't think this game is an experience for everyone, i feel like Limbus Company is probably the best game to start on this universe and get accostumed to it, and then, if you love it, then you should try Library of Ruina (and maybe use a mod or guides if u can't handle the combat and only wants to get the peak story, idk, i feel like the combat and actually playing it is a big factor of what makes this game special, but it's ur call), then going to Lobotomy Corporation, that i actually didn't like too much, but I should probably go back there to see if I like it more now

If i would give my score based on how good the game is comparting pros and cons, it's score would probably be an 8 or something
However, this is my personal experience, I am a big fan of PM, i played the game before and after, and i enjoy turn based combat, so i'm gonna push it up "a bit"

That's that, and this is this.
9.25/10
The game is difficult but the story is worth it. You should play this game with a guide to help mitigate the huge difficulty spikes in the game.

Binah has me on a chain, perfect game for puppy girls

I must say that Library of Ruina is a tad bit harder than Lobotomy Corporation, it's quite fun, the visuals feel nice and we get a good amount of world building, the gameplay compared to Limbus feels smoother and more straightforward. It's understandable how LoR is the favourite project moon game for a good chunk of the fandom, would recommend for Limbus only players as it builds a strong foundation towards the City unlike LC which is more focused towards L corp and the Crew (not to say that people shouldn't play it, it's my preferred Pm game).

Finally tried a project moon game after avoiding it for so long, and i see why the game got the reputation that it has. The Story, Settings, World Building, and Characters are really good, and the music and gameplay are just top tier. Gameplay especially has a lot of depth that i did not anticipate coming in. 

A Definite recommendation for anyone that loves a difficult deckbuilder with a lot of emphasis on story telling and world building.

Amazing story with good worldbuilding and amazing characters with their own respective philosophies! The combat system's really in-depth and you can see the influence it had on their future work, Limbus Company. Unfortunately, it has an abysmal difficulty that just feels hard. Not fun-hard or dark-souls hard. Just hard.

I love this game, mostly the story, worldbuilding, and characters. I will do something terrible if I keep minimum rolling while my opponents max roll. 

Also, the game gets kinda difficult near the end, but it was fun regardless.

Yeah its good. Play if you like deckbuilder and story elements. Quite a unique game.

pretty fun game, love deck builders and project moon :D

This is a big shift if you're coming from Lobotomy Corp, though for Limbus Company fans it won't be too far off. The difficulty is pretty linear in the beginning of the game but later becomes vertical, you will lose resceptions, you will lose clashes, your librarians will die. But it's up to your ability to make the game harder or easier based on what you use and how you use it. If you're a fan of Project Moon games or just a fan of Deckbuilding turn based fighting games, you should absolutely give the game a try.

pretty good. Like favorite game of all time pretty good. combat's solid. story is great. the default music on the deckbuilding screen is burned into the side of my brain. all around worth playing, and if you have the patience for it try lobotomy corp.

I really enjoyed some aspects of this game, like the world, the various characters, and the story. The gameplay, however, is:
1. Confusing at the beginning, as the tutorials are rather poor;
2. Pretty engaging and interesting in the mid-game, and yet;
3. An absolute slog towards the end-game.

I believe there are multiple reasons for this. First - the UI during combat is just.. bad. You need to constantly hover over each enemy action to know what they’re playing, while also going through every teammate to see what you can play, and all in all, when it’s a 5v5 battle, each turn just ends up taking way too long. I really think the combat should've been capped at 3v3.
The other main reason is that the enemies just have too many passives that are way too long, so each start of combat, you just have to sit down and read for 5 mins; make it 10 mins for the puzzle-like abnormality encounters, most of which basically boil down to “kill this one first“. And after all that, the game still makes the player go through a "boss rush" of those abnormality fights, which I personally found annoying, as I feel like I already had "solved the puzzles".

Music is rather inconsistent; absolute bangers in some places, and repetitive, out of place stuff in others. The art suffers a bit too - some characters are way more detailed, and it's almost as if the artists hadn't agreed on a shared style to follow.

Tras 170 horas he completado este juego al 100%
En mi opinión, aunque la historia puede llegar a ser algo confusa si no tienes conocimiento previo del universo de Project Moon, aún así considero que está MUY bien escrita y contiene personajes bastante profundos con psicologías distintas para cada uno de ellos.
En momentos el gameplay me resultó frustrante o complicado de entender, pero una vez logras entender cómo jugar y utilizar las mecánicas a tu favor, varias de las peleas pueden volverse bastante entrenenidas por realmente tener que pensar bien tus movimientos.
El soundtrack no decepciona para nada, realmente la música hace cada momento más intenso. Mis canciones favoritas del soundtrack son los temas de las realizaciones y las canciones de Mili, en especial Iron lotus y Poems of a machine.

También que la robot esta buenota, y me gustan los robots entonces si el juego tiene un robot buenote automáticamente se vuelve bueno 10/10 toma pibe +1 😂 buena

HOWEVER there are so many difficulty cliffs at different areas that make you wonder how anyone can beat this game legitimately, if youre going to play this game and want an easier time than the base difficulty grab free passive from the workshop. if you want to play even easier, grab ether super finn or the reception of son goku, these will give you pages that break the game
